creator: John Allspaw

evidence.locator: primary page, introduction and paragraphs under 'The Questions Are As Important As The Answers'
evidence.text: Etsy says it continued to explore and evolve how it learns from accidents, then open-sourced a facilitation guide. It treats postmortem debriefing first as a learning opportunity and says organizational learning requires objective event data to be placed in context with subjective data gathered from multiple perspectives.

provenance: Primary Etsy Code as Craft publication manually re-reviewed from blocked saturation discovery on 2026-07-12.
relevance_evidence: Etsy documents its evolving, organization-specific debriefing practice and the mechanism used to turn incidents into shared learning.
retrieval_method: manual primary-page review via public web reader
rights_note: Only a bounded paraphrased evidence span is stored; no complete third-party page is copied.
title: Etsy's Debriefing Facilitation Guide for Blameless Postmortems
